heroTitle: What I offer. What it does.
heroDesc: Each therapy I offer draws on distinct but complementary clinical traditions. I combine them according to what your situation requires — not one framework applied to everyone.
herbalP1: Western herbal medicine is the foundation of my practice. It uses medicinal plants — roots, leaves, flowers, bark — to support and restore the health of body and mind.
herbalP2: My approach is rooted in the European phytotherapy tradition, with complementary training in Traditional Chinese Medicine and Ayurveda. I draw on the most appropriate tools for each situation rather than applying a single framework to everyone.
herbalP3: Each initial consultation results in a personalised written protocol — a herbal formulation designed for you, naturopathic recommendations, and if necessary, contact with a quality supplier for your preparation.
naturoP1: Naturopathy seeks to identify and address the root causes of health problems — not just the symptoms. It draws on nutrition, lifestyle, detox support, stress management and environmental adjustments.
naturoP2: In my practice, naturopathy is always integrated with herbal medicine. Plants and lifestyle form a whole — each supports the other for more lasting results.
ihmQuote: "They leave feeling like they are floating."
ihmP1: Indian head massage — rooted in the Ayurvedic tradition — works on the back, neck, shoulders, scalp and face. It releases accumulated muscle tension, calms the nervous system and induces a state of deep wellbeing.
ihmP2: Ideal for chronic headaches, neck tension, insomnia, mental overload, or simply as a deep recovery pause. Some people describe an effect that lasts for days.
athleteP1: I trained at national level in artistic swimming. I understand what it means to live in a performance body — its demands, its logic, and what it feels like when it stops working the way it should.
athleteP2: My approach for athletes covers adaptogens and physiological stress management, recovery support and inflammation reduction, hormonal disruption linked to intense training, and support during injury or rehabilitation phases.
